item.title: Bronson Watermarker goes Pro
item.description:
Bronson Watermarker Pro, our app for generating personalized watermarked documents, is out today. This all-new version adds support for images (nearly any format) in addition to PDFs, along with many quality-of-life enhancements for professional users. Like most of the apps we make, Bronson exists because of a very specific pain point I encountered. While developing […]

The post Bronson Watermarker goes Pro first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
Bronson Watermarker Pro, our app for generating personalized watermarked documents, is out today. This all-new version adds support for images (nearly any format) in addition to PDFs, along with many quality-of-life enhancements for professional users. Like most of the apps we make, Bronson exists because of a very specific pain point I encountered. While developing \[…\]
The post [Bronson Watermarker goes Pro](https://johnaugust.com/2026/bronson-watermarker-goes-pro) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: Update: Elsevier is Suing Meta For Whom? (And will we ever really know?): Some thoughts on copyright class actions claiming to represent authors
item.description: As we wrote in May, Elsevier is suing Meta over use of copyrighted works for training Meta’s AI models. It has a set of claims very similar to those in Kadrey v. Meta, but with a different purported class that includes authors and publishers who own copyright in academic journal articles and other academic works.
item.source:markdown: As we wrote in May, Elsevier is suing Meta over use of copyrighted works for training Meta’s AI models. It has a set of claims very similar to those in Kadrey v. Meta, but with a different purported class that includes authors and publishers who own copyright in academic journal articles and other academic works.

item.title: Writing while the World is on Fire (Encore)
item.description:
How do you keep doing creative work when the world is falling apart around you? To sift through the despair and doubt, John welcomes back legendary Scriptnotes guest, writer-turned-psychotherapist Dennis Palumbo. They discuss the many feelings that catastrophic events can bring up in artists, the personal narratives that often inform those feelings, and how to […]

The post Writing while the World is on Fire (Encore) first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
How do you keep doing creative work when the world is falling apart around you? To sift through the despair and doubt, John welcomes back legendary Scriptnotes guest, writer-turned-psychotherapist Dennis Palumbo. They discuss the many feelings that catastrophic events can bring up in artists, the personal narratives that often inform those feelings, and how to \[…\]
The post [Writing while the World is on Fire (Encore)](https://johnaugust.com/2026/writing-while-the-world-is-on-fire-encore) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: Frontier models are surprisingly bad at jigsaw puzzles
item.description:
Every week, we see new stories of how capable the frontier AI models are becoming. They’re not just generating text and images. They’re making breakthroughs on mathematical conjectures and acing exams. Models like Mythos and Fable can work autonomously for hours to diagnose (and potentially exploit) cybersecurity flaws. But as of July 2026, they’re surprisingly […]

The post Frontier models are surprisingly bad at jigsaw puzzles first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
Every week, we see new stories of how capable the frontier AI models are becoming. They’re not just generating text and images. They’re making breakthroughs on mathematical conjectures and acing exams. Models like Mythos and Fable can work autonomously for hours to diagnose (and potentially exploit) cybersecurity flaws. But as of July 2026, they’re surprisingly \[…\]
The post [Frontier models are surprisingly bad at jigsaw puzzles](https://johnaugust.com/2026/ai-jigsaw-puzzles) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: Our Comments in the OMB Rulemaking
item.description: Last month, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) issued a notice seeking comment on a proposed new rule. The rule would make highly consequential changes to the Uniform Guidance for Federal Financial Assistance. At last count, OMB had received nearly than 350,000 comments, and more than 50,000 were available to read. Today we filed ours.
item.source:markdown: Last month, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) issued a notice seeking comment on a proposed new rule. The rule would make highly consequential changes to the Uniform Guidance for Federal Financial Assistance. At last count, OMB had received nearly than 350,000 comments, and more than 50,000 were available to read. Today we filed ours.

item.title: Thinking on Your Feet with Ryan Condal
item.description:
John and Craig sit down with Ryan Condal, showrunner of House of the Dragon, just as its third season airs. They dive into Ryan’s late start in Hollywood, spec scripts, the anatomy of a writers room, and thinking as a product of movement. They also answer a listener question about the mechanics of writing a […]

The post Thinking on Your Feet with Ryan Condal first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
John and Craig sit down with Ryan Condal, showrunner of House of the Dragon, just as its third season airs. They dive into Ryan’s late start in Hollywood, spec scripts, the anatomy of a writers room, and thinking as a product of movement. They also answer a listener question about the mechanics of writing a \[…\]
The post [Thinking on Your Feet with Ryan Condal](https://johnaugust.com/2026/thinking-on-your-feet-with-ryan-condal) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: IFComp 2026 Now Accepting Intents & Entries
item.description:
IFComp 2026 Now Accepting Intents & Entries

Hello, everyone, and happy (slightly belated) start of July! With a new July comes a new season of the Interactive Fiction Competition!

From now through August 1st 2026, 11:59pm Eastern (23:59 ET), the IF Comp website will be open for authors to declare their intent to enter this year’s competition!

Final entries are due on August 28th, 11:59pm Eastern, but you must register by August 1st!

If you miss it, there’s always 2027… If you register and then can’t complete your entry in time, you may always back out of the competition and enter your work elsewhere or next year.

As with the previous iterations of the IFComp, authors will be allowed to participate as judges, vote, and review entries other than their own.

If you have any questions about the competition or its rules, you can contact us at ifcomp@ifcomp.org

In addition to entries, we are also accepting prizes to award contestants! If you would like to donate a prize for this year’s competition, you can email us at prizes@ifcomp.org — no prize is too humble or too grand.

If you would prefer to donate money, our Colossal Fundraiser will launch by August. Another announcement will be made then.

Thank you, everyone. We’re looking forward to another fun year.
item.source:markdown:
# [IFComp 2026 Now Accepting Intents & Entries](https://blog.ifcomp.org/post/788067590629146624/ifcomp-2025-now-accepting-intents-entries)
Hello, everyone, and happy (slightly belated) start of July! With a new July comes a new season of the Interactive Fiction Competition!
## **From now through _August 1st_ 2026, 11:59pm Eastern (23:59 ET), the [IF Comp website](https://href.li/?https://ifcomp.org) will be open for authors to declare their intent to enter this year’s competition!**
Final entries are due on **August 28th**, 11:59pm Eastern, but you must register by **August 1st**!
If you miss it, there’s always 2027… If you register and then can’t complete your entry in time, you may always back out of the competition and enter your work elsewhere or next year.
As with the previous iterations of the IFComp, authors will be allowed to participate as judges, vote, and review entries other than their own.
If you have any questions about the competition or its rules, you can contact us at [ifcomp@ifcomp.org](https://mail.google.com/mail/?view=cm&fs=1&tf=1&to=ifcomp@ifcomp.org)
In addition to entries, **we are also accepting prizes to award contestants!** If you would like to donate a prize for this year’s competition, you can email us at [prizes@ifcomp.org](https://mail.google.com/mail/?view=cm&fs=1&tf=1&to=prizes@ifcomp.org) — no prize is too humble or too grand.
If you would prefer to donate money, our Colossal Fundraiser will launch by August. Another announcement will be made then.
Thank you, everyone. We’re looking forward to another fun year.

item.title: Vanishing Culture Episode #1: What We Stand to Lose
item.description: Authors Alliance and Internet Archive are excited to announce the release of the first episode of Vanishing Culture, a special six-part series from the Future Knowledge podcast exploring what happens when our shared cultural heritage disappears, and what we can do to preserve it.
item.source:markdown: Authors Alliance and Internet Archive are excited to announce the release of the first episode of Vanishing Culture, a special six-part series from the Future Knowledge podcast exploring what happens when our shared cultural heritage disappears, and what we can do to preserve it.

item.title: Obsession with Curry Barker
item.description:
John and Craig welcome Curry Barker, the writer-director behind this summer’s surprise blockbuster, Obsession. It’s a rom-com, a horror film and a great example of trusting your audience. We discuss Curry’s roots, his writing process, learning from YouTube, as well as the pressure to follow up a hit. In our bonus segment for premium subscribers, […]

The post Obsession with Curry Barker first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
John and Craig welcome Curry Barker, the writer-director behind this summer’s surprise blockbuster, Obsession. It’s a rom-com, a horror film and a great example of trusting your audience. We discuss Curry’s roots, his writing process, learning from YouTube, as well as the pressure to follow up a hit. In our bonus segment for premium subscribers, \[…\]
The post [Obsession with Curry Barker](https://johnaugust.com/2026/obsession-with-curry-barker) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).
